strengths: Exceeds tenure requirement with 15+ years of full-stack development; current Staff-level position demonstrates career progression and depth
Core stack verified in production: TypeScript/Node.js/React at Optilyz (millions of annual transactions) and re:cinq; MongoDB expertise; Docker and CI/CD demonstrated
Testing expertise embedded throughout career; TDD methodology applied consistently; organized testing courses and workshops; clear quality-first mindset
concerns: Nest.js completely absent from resume despite being the primary backend framework specified for the role; zero mention in employment history or skill list raises immediate productivity concerns
Java/Kotlin experience is 6–11 years old (last positions 2015–2018); resume shows decisive pivot to TypeScript/Node.js with no recent secondary-stack work, leaving that requirement undemonstrated

Testing and database toolchain specifics unmet: Vitest and Playwright not mentioned despite being named primary testing stack; PostgreSQL (specifically required database) not referenced; no explicit security/auth pattern expertise highlighted
response.justification: Szabo's career demonstrates exceptional full-stack and architectural scope, but critical gaps in the specified tech stack undermine fit for this role. Nest.js—the primary backend framework—is entirely absent from the employment record and skill list. Recent work focuses exclusively on TypeScript/Node.js, leaving Java and Kotlin (required secondary languages) 6–11 years stale. Testing expertise exists but not with the specified toolchain (Vitest, Playwright); PostgreSQL is not demonstrated; security/auth patterns are never mentioned. The resume portrays a generalist with infrastructure and open-source strengths, not a specialist prepared for the immediate productivity demands of this team's specific technology stack.
